In the mean time, I’ll be looking forward to your feedback and questions via comments here in this post.

entry.title: Cycle Counting Physical Inventory in SAP WM at Bin Level
entry.content:
Introduction- In cycle counting inventory, you enter your material stocks physically at regular intervals within a fiscal year. By dividing materials into various classes (for example, A, B, C, D, and so on), you specify for those materials at which intervals or cycles the inventory is to be performed. This allows you to make an inventory fast-moving materials in your warehouse more often than slow-moving material.

Cycle counting is basically the process of continually validation the accuracy of the inventory in the warehouse by regularly counting a portion of the inventory on a daily or weekly basis. Every item in the warehouse is counted at lease several times in a year.

Cycle Counting can be performed at IM level as well as WM Level.

In WM  We can go for two types of Cycle Counting Physical Inventory

1- Cycle Counting at Bin Level

2- Cycle Counting at Quant Level 

In this blog I am going to write about Cycle Counting at Bin Level

WM Level Cycle Counting Configuration

  • ABC  indicator has to be maintained in Material Management

Navigation SPRO-Material Management-Inventory Management and Physical Inventory-Physical Inventory-Cycle Counting

Master Data Set UP at IM Level

Set Cycle Counting Indicator (M1BC)

Navigation Logistics – Material Management- Physical Inventory- Special Procedures-Cycle Counting – Set Cycle Counting Indicator

Click change CC Indicator

It can be checked in MM03

 

Cycle Counting Configuration in WM for Storage Type

Navigation SPRO-Logistic Execution- Warehouse Management – Activity – Physical Inventory -Define Types per storage type

Process Flow

Create Cycle Counting Document in WM

Transaction Code LX26

Count the inventory

Transaction code – LI11n

Input Counted quantity In put here lesser than actual

And save the count

Inventory Count Added

Clear difference in WM

Transaction LI20

Write Off the count

Clear difference at IM level

Transaction code LI21 or LX23

I am taking here LI21

After write off  Material document will be created

Check LX03

One pallet quantity was 0 so it disappeared

Other pallet quantity 1 and it was increased by 1 quantity so it is changed

After the entire physical inventory is complete for the fiscal year, you reset the inventory data for storage bins and storage quants using the RLREOLPQ report. In the next inventory period, you can then perform a new physical inventory for these storage bins.

Note- You should not reset the quant data, since the reference to the inventory date is then lost, and the system proposes too many bins for your next cycle counting inventory.

Reference - https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/b2dee5e83e2446149294f9860a7c08f0/8f91c95360267214e10000000a174cb4.html

Please share your feedback so I can improve it 

 

Regards

Rashid

entry.title: Cycle Counting Physical Inventory in SAP WM at Quant Level
entry.content:
Hi All

I wrote blog previously about Cycle Counting at Bin Level https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/cycle-counting-physical-inventory-in-sap-wm-at-bin-level/ba-p/13938236. In this blog I am going to write about Cycle Counting at Quant Level in WM.

Introduction- In contrast to the Cycle Counting Inventory, the cycle counting inventory at quant level is not based on the storage bin, but rather on the individual quants in your warehouse.

If you use cycle counting inventory at quant level, the system does not set the inventory block for the entire storage bin. It sets the block instead for the quant to be inventoried. You count only the selected materials, not all materials in the storage bin. If you manage storage bins with mixed storage, these storage bins are accessible for stock movements with other quants. There can be open transfer orders for all materials that were not flagged for cycle counting in the inventory document. You can also create transfer orders for these materials during the inventory. The system saves the inventory data at quant level.

All Configuration Steps will be same I mentioned in my Previous Blog https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/cycle-counting-physical-inventory-in-sap-wm-at-bin-level/ba-p/13938236 Except below additional configuration,

In Customizing - SPRO- Logistic Execution-Warehouse Management- Activities- Physical Inventory- Define Types per Storage Type ,

 

you have specified the inventory procedure for the required storage type as continuous inventory using cycle counting.

 

SPRO-Logistic Execution- Warehouse Management -Activities- Physical Inventory -Maintain Number Ranges for Inventory number range Quant

Process Step

To create physical inventory documents at quant level for the cycle counting inventory

Logistics Execution- Internal Whse Processes - Physical Inventory - In Warehouse Management Physical Inventory Document - Cycle Counting per Quant
Transaction Code LICC

Note- If you generate inventory documents for the cycle counting inventory at quant level, these documents are active immediately . In this case, you cannot schedule the physical inventory in advance by first generating the inventory documents and activating them at a later point.The system immediately blocks the affected quants for all other stock movements.

For Rest Process Step we can refer last Blog https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/cycle-counting-physical-inventory-in-sap-wm-at-bin-level/ba-p/13938236

Count LI11N

WM Level Difference LI20

IM Level Difference LI21 or LX23

To obtain an evaluation about the progress of the cycle counting

Logistics Execution  Internal Whse Processes  Information System  Warehouse  Physical Inventory  With Bin Inventory Management   Evaluation of Quant Inventory
Transaction Code LI15

Note- In a stock movement, if you move a quant from one storage bin to another, the system deletes the quant in the source storage bin and creates a new quant in the destination storage bin. Because the system stores the inventory data for the cycle counting inventory at quant level in the quant data, any inventory data that you previously entered is lost in this type of stock transfer.

Conclusion- This method is very useful. we dont want to block all stock or bin for a particular material so we can use it by blocking a quant. It will help us to run business smoothly without impacting.

Please share your feedback to improve it 

References- https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/b2dee5e83e2446149294f9860a7c08f0/278ec95360267214e10000000a174cb4.html

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/cycle-counting-physical-inventory-in-sap-wm-at-bin-level/ba-p/13938236 

 

Thanks

entry.title: Concept for Warehouse Billing in EWM
entry.content:
Hi All

I am going to explain warehouse billing Concept in EWM.

Introduction- In EWM, we can record the quantity of warehouse services used for the services agreed upon in an agreement in the Transportation Management. We can subsequently send this quantity information back to the TM for charge calculation and settlement based on the charges you have agreed to in the agreement

Benefits-

1- Sell warehouse services to customers and bill the customers periodically based the services used for a time period

2- Purchase warehouse services from external service providers and periodically pay the service provider based on the warehouse services used for a time period.

Available Features in Warehouse Billing

1- Snapshot management
2- Warehouse billing measurement request management
3- Warehouse billing measurement management

We will describe each of one 

1- Snapshot Management - We use this feature to capture the EWM business data relevant to measuring warehouse services and for calculating the charges for billing

We can record below mentioned business data in snapshot management

1- Snapshot of Physical Stock - We create a snapshot for stock at a particular time. The system records the warehouse inventory data at this particular time. We can create snapshots to record the presence of physical stock on a daily basis. If We create multiple snapshots in one day, the system merges the physical stock on that specific date.

2- Snapshot for Inbound Deliveries - The system records business data for goods that have been received and confirmed in Inbound Delivery Document (Document Type PDI). If the inbound delivery items are partially received, the item is recorded partially with the quantity

3- Snapshot for Outbound Deliveries-The system records business data for goods that have been sent and have a completed status in Outbound Delivery Document (Document Type FDO).

4- Snapshot for Warehouse tasks-The system records data for warehouse tasks that have been carried out and confirmed

We below Scenarios to perform 

 1- We can Create Snapshots to create snapshots on a daily basis
2- Configure Snapshots to activate/deactivate, and specify other settings for the snapshots
3- To create snapshots using specify attributes, we can create snapshots for Physical stock, Inbound               deliveries, Outbound deliveries and warehouse tasks.

We can delete and remove snapshots

2- Request for Warehouse Billing Measurement- EWM automatically creates a request for warehouse billing measurement (WBMR) from the Transportation Management (TM) agreement data. In the WBMR, we can assign warehouse billing measurement services to measure the warehouse services used, and periodically create warehouse billing measurements (WBMs) for calculating the quantity of services used for a billing period. The system uses the WBM information for calculating charges for the warehouse services used in the TM. When agreement information in TM is changed, the WBMR reflects the changes in the EWM.

WBMR Status

In Process
Activated
Deactivated

When you save and release an agreement in TM, EWM automatically receives the agreement as a request for warehouse billing measurement (WBMR). The WBMR has the same billing schedule determined in the agreement in TM. The billing schedule refers to a specified factory calendar both in TM and EWM. You must specify the same factory calendar settings in both the systems.

Warehouse Billing Measurement Service Assignment 

We can calculate the quantity of warehouse services used after you assign a warehouse billing measurement service to an item in a request for warehouse billing measurement document. We can measure the service quantity for the following EWM documents
Number of documents

Product quantity

Weight

Volume

Number of bins

Number of handling units

We can also specify a variant along with the warehouse billing measurement service in the WBMR item to enter specific constraints to measure warehouse services. For example, we can constrain the measurement to a specific group of documents, products, bins, or handling unit.

Note-The system subsequently measures the quantity from EWM business data in snapshots

3- Warehouse Billing Measurement Management-

After we have activated a request for billing measurement, we can display the generated warehouse billing measurements (WBM) in the Warehouse Management Monitor under Billing Warehouse Billing Measurement. The WBM contains the quantity of warehouse services used. A WBM can be generated for every billing period we have defined. The quantity in the WBM is obtained from the business data recorded into the snapshots. In the Warehouse Management Monitor, you can see the business data that was used to calculate the quantity as the proof of services.

Billing measurement document has following statuses

Ready for Distribution
Distributed
No Distribution

Reporting for Warehouse Billing in EWM Warehouse Monitor

For reporing we can use warehouse management monitor-Billing-Snapshots
For reporting we can use warehouse management monitor - Billing - Request for Warehouse Billing Measurement.

For reporing we can use warehouse management monitor-Billing-Billing Measurement

 

Please share your feedback to improve it 

 

Reference-https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/9832125c23154a179bfa1784cdc9577a/5ad63855b04c6a38e10000000a174cb4.html

 

 

Regerads

Rashid
